title: Add starting point for podman system testing

comments.body:
On 07/18/2018 03:59 PM, Daniel Walsh wrote:
> On 07/18/2018 02:08 PM, Chris Evich wrote:
>> s'pose there's really nothin' to it, but to do it...
>>
> Yes exactly.
>
> Currently we have found 4 bugs in podman in rhel8.
> 
> Bad containers-common
> 
> Bad runc
> 
> Bad containersnetworking
> 
> Bad podman
> 
> Relying on this working in Fedora 28 is not good enough.
> 

There...climbed the giant 'get started' hill :smile: 

@edsantiago @rhatdan PTAL
Adding unittests for the systemtests, made those part of the main unittesting (not sure if it should go elsewhere).  I'm not happy with the python file names, open to suggestions.

@cevich

Naming: I would go with test_version.py and TestVersion
Super class: I would push fmt_popen in a subclass of unittest.TestCase and then use that class as a super class for your unittests.

I'll also point out the python library and go library have good coverage on the unit/integration side of things so I would keep these system tests non-exhaustive on options but rather are the rpm's built correctly and in the right combination of packages.  Image/Container/Pod CRUD, if it works for one pass it should for all.  The lower level testing should catch option combinations, or be enhanced to do so if they don't today. 

https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/1106 is going to have merge conflicts with your Makefile changes.  I don't think the others changes in that PR will impact you since you're calling podman as an executable.

Thanks @jwhonce Yeah, that was my original class-hierarchy, but with only one test, it seemed unnecessary at this stage.  Same for the subject-matter, I just picked something basically guaranteed to pass. I like your suggestions though, and that's the general direction I'd like to take in future PRs:

1. Add a module(s) to contain ``unittest.TestCase`` super-classes w/ utility/helper methods.
2. Add a single test to exercise all the primary workflow sub-commands:  **pull, build, run, kill, rm**.
3. Add tests to cover important/critical options to each podman sub-command (e.g. ``podman run -v`` and ``-e``)
4. Add in-depth tests for less used, or known-buggy workflows and/or options.

We're going to have some overlap with the integration tests, but we should keep that to a minimum.  Focusing on common combinations of podman sub-commands and options, then going more in-depth later on.

Re: #1106 Noted, thanks.  I'll take a peek and modify accordingly.

So you think ``test_version.py`` and ``unittest_version.py`` are okay then?  I toyed briefly with making separate sub-modules (directories) to keep things organized, but I realized that can also be done later.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 9a18681) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

@jwhonce @rhatdan okay, got this cleaned up and working at a basic/bootstrapping level.  I left out running the ``systemtest`` target in CI for now, it can be added/enabled later if needed.  PTAL.

I also did some minor cleanup work on the ``.travis.yml`` file.  Now you can actually see which job is testing what in travis by ``COMMENT=foobar``
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 1387bc9) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

Hi @cevich I prefer to implement this with ginkgo. I start a pullreq with your test cases 

https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1385

It is quite simple and only with the version test. Can you have a loook at it?
(updated with @jwhonce suggestion + added ``--verbose`` to all new system tests)
okay, I think this PR is ready for merge, assuming @ypu is on-board with the plan (above).

Hi @cevich 
The first step of restructure is done. And now I am working on the unittest for the common functions.  It may takes me one or two days to finish it. 
If this is OK for you, maybe we can hold this pullreq for a minute to see if the ginkgo system test still need a big change. 

But if you want to make this pullreq merged to make it as a system test stub in the workflow as quick as possible, I also understand it. So it's depends on you. :)

@ypu a few days is not a problem, weeks/months is too long.  I'm afraid about the complexity and size of your PR vs. this one.  I try to make it very small, targeted, specific and simple.

Is there a way to  make your PR like this?  Maybe start from version-test perspective, only include library dependencies for it, and drop others (they can come by more PRs).

@cevich Golang unit test for a separated function is very easy. The unit test for struct and interface may a little complex. I am trying to keep that part simple. Left some functions for PodmanTest struct to e2e test, the same for some update to the functions. Looking forward to your review for next version. And if I can not get that PR ready next week. Please go ahead to merge this one. Don't want the PR block your following tasks.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #818) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
